data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="helix-1940a77b">
    <title>Add first-class contract artifact support in HELIX design phase</title>
    <description>
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Hugo version: 0.159.2 extended — pinned in .github/workflows/pages.yml · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading) · Agent harness: ddx agent run --harness claude — not claude -p</practices>
<governing>workflows/phases/02-design/README.md — HELIX design docs, artifact templates, and phase guidance include a first-class contract artifact with explicit selection rules and naming guidance</governing>
</context-digest>

HELIX design guidance already treats contracts as a first-class artifact category (`docs/helix/02-design/contracts/`), and the design gate expects contract docs to exist, but the workflow bundle does not currently ship a dedicated contract artifact template/prompt/meta alongside solution-design, technical-design, ADR, spike, and PoC templates. At the same time, live HELIX repos are using both `API-*` and `CONTRACT-*` identifiers for documents in the contracts directory. This leaves contract-shaped specs underspecified exactly when we need them for reusable protocol/interface definitions such as OpenTelemetry telemetry capture standards, DDx/HELIX boundary contracts, execution-document conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other cross-project schemas that should be shareable without dragging along feature-level or solution-level baggage.

Request: add an explicit first-class contract artifact type to HELIX design-phase tooling and documentation. The artifact must be suitable for normative external interface specifications, not just HTTP APIs. It should support protocol/data/interface contracts such as telemetry schemas, cross-project ownership boundaries, authoring conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other reusable contracts. The artifact definition should make clear that ADRs carry the decision/rationale (WHY), solution designs carry the feature-level approach (HOW for a feature), technical designs carry one bounded slice, and contracts carry the authoritative field-by-field/schema-level/interface-level specification that another team could implement against directly.

Requirements from this discussion:
1. Define clear selection rules for when to use ADR vs Solution Design vs Technical Design vs Contract.
2. Ship a contract template/prompt/meta in the HELIX workflow bundle.
3. Clarify identifier and naming guidance: whether `API-*` remains a subtype, whether `CONTRACT-*` is the canonical prefix, or whether both are supported with explicit scope boundaries.
4. Make the contract artifact suitable for non-HTTP surfaces, including telemetry/export contracts.
5. Require contracts to carry exact keys/fields, types, units, enums, precedence rules, formulas, error semantics, examples, and validation checklists where relevant.
6. Ensure the design gate/enforcer language matches the chosen artifact naming and template support.
7. Preserve the ability for contracts to be shared across projects without inheriting unrelated feature or solution baggage.
8. Document how contracts should be referenced from ADRs and solution designs so the normative schema lives in one place rather than being duplicated across artifacts.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
HELIX design docs, artifact templates, and phase guidance include a first-class contract artifact with explicit selection rules and naming guidance; contract template support exists in the workflow bundle; guidance covers non-HTTP protocol/interface contracts such as telemetry schemas; ADR/SD/TD/contract boundaries are unambiguous enough that a telemetry export contract can be placed without artifact-taxonomy confusion
    </acceptance>
    <labels>helix, phase:design, kind:spec, area:docs, area:workflow</labels>
    <metadata parent="helix-13cfe23f" spec-id="workflows/phases/02-design/README.md" base-rev="7f97067b3541319cac3ee3889691912844d95ae3" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="workflows/phases/02-design/README.md" path="workflows/phases/02-design/README.md"/>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read file contents. Use instead of cat/head/tail.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

offset.description: 0-based line offset to start reading from

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return

tools.name: write
tools.description: Create or overwrite a file with the given content. Creates parent directories as needed.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file using exact string replacement.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

oldText.description: Exact text to find (must be unique in the file and must not overlap with other edits)

old_string.description: Legacy: exact string to find (use edits[] instead)

tools.name: bash
tools.description: Execute a shell command for targeted repo operations such as builds, tests, or git. Do not use this for file discovery or file reading; use the find, grep, ls, and read tools instead. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.

tools.name: find
tools.description: Find files by path pattern. Use this instead of shell find or ls when locating files by name.

pattern.description: Path pattern to match (e.g. '**/*.go', 'cmd/**/main.go'). Use ** to match across directories.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: grep
tools.description: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Use instead of grep/rg shell commands.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative or absolute; defaults to working directory)

glob.description: Restrict to files whose base names match this glob (e.g. '*.go', '*.ts')

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: ls
tools.description: List directory contents. Use instead of ls.

path.description: Directory to list (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

tools.name: patch
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file. Supports: replace (default), replace_all, prepend, and append operations.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

search.description: Exact text to find in the file. Must match exactly (including whitespace). Will match first occurrence.

content.description: Replacement text, or text to prepend/append

operation.description: One of: 'replace' (default, search must be unique), 'replace_all' (replace every occurrence), 'prepend' (insert at file start), 'append' (insert at file end)

tools.name: task
tools.description: Track subtasks in a multi-step plan: create, update, get, list.

id.description: Task ID (required for update/get, ignored for create/list)

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file using exact string replacement.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

oldText.description: Exact text to find (must be unique in the file and must not overlap with other edits)

old_string.description: Legacy: exact string to find (use edits[] instead)

tools.name: bash
tools.description: Execute a shell command for targeted repo operations such as builds, tests, or git. Do not use this for file discovery or file reading; use the find, grep, ls, and read tools instead. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.

tools.name: find
tools.description: Find files by path pattern. Use this instead of shell find or ls when locating files by name.

pattern.description: Path pattern to match (e.g. '**/*.go', 'cmd/**/main.go'). Use ** to match across directories.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: grep
tools.description: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Use instead of grep/rg shell commands.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative or absolute; defaults to working directory)

glob.description: Restrict to files whose base names match this glob (e.g. '*.go', '*.ts')

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: ls
tools.description: List directory contents. Use instead of ls.

path.description: Directory to list (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

tools.name: patch
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file. Supports: replace (default), replace_all, prepend, and append operations.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

search.description: Exact text to find in the file. Must match exactly (including whitespace). Will match first occurrence.

content.description: Replacement text, or text to prepend/append

operation.description: One of: 'replace' (default, search must be unique), 'replace_all' (replace every occurrence), 'prepend' (insert at file start), 'append' (insert at file end)

tools.name: task
tools.description: Track subtasks in a multi-step plan: create, update, get, list.

id.description: Task ID (required for update/get, ignored for create/list)

messages.content:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="helix-1940a77b">
    <title>Add first-class contract artifact support in HELIX design phase</title>
    <description>
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Hugo version: 0.159.2 extended — pinned in .github/workflows/pages.yml · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading) · Agent harness: ddx agent run --harness claude — not claude -p</practices>
<governing>workflows/phases/02-design/README.md — HELIX design docs, artifact templates, and phase guidance include a first-class contract artifact with explicit selection rules and naming guidance</governing>
</context-digest>

HELIX design guidance already treats contracts as a first-class artifact category (`docs/helix/02-design/contracts/`), and the design gate expects contract docs to exist, but the workflow bundle does not currently ship a dedicated contract artifact template/prompt/meta alongside solution-design, technical-design, ADR, spike, and PoC templates. At the same time, live HELIX repos are using both `API-*` and `CONTRACT-*` identifiers for documents in the contracts directory. This leaves contract-shaped specs underspecified exactly when we need them for reusable protocol/interface definitions such as OpenTelemetry telemetry capture standards, DDx/HELIX boundary contracts, execution-document conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other cross-project schemas that should be shareable without dragging along feature-level or solution-level baggage.

Request: add an explicit first-class contract artifact type to HELIX design-phase tooling and documentation. The artifact must be suitable for normative external interface specifications, not just HTTP APIs. It should support protocol/data/interface contracts such as telemetry schemas, cross-project ownership boundaries, authoring conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other reusable contracts. The artifact definition should make clear that ADRs carry the decision/rationale (WHY), solution designs carry the feature-level approach (HOW for a feature), technical designs carry one bounded slice, and contracts carry the authoritative field-by-field/schema-level/interface-level specification that another team could implement against directly.

Requirements from this discussion:
1. Define clear selection rules for when to use ADR vs Solution Design vs Technical Design vs Contract.
2. Ship a contract template/prompt/meta in the HELIX workflow bundle.
3. Clarify identifier and naming guidance: whether `API-*` remains a subtype, whether `CONTRACT-*` is the canonical prefix, or whether both are supported with explicit scope boundaries.
4. Make the contract artifact suitable for non-HTTP surfaces, including telemetry/export contracts.
5. Require contracts to carry exact keys/fields, types, units, enums, precedence rules, formulas, error semantics, examples, and validation checklists where relevant.
6. Ensure the design gate/enforcer language matches the chosen artifact naming and template support.
7. Preserve the ability for contracts to be shared across projects without inheriting unrelated feature or solution baggage.
8. Document how contracts should be referenced from ADRs and solution designs so the normative schema lives in one place rather than being duplicated across artifacts.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
HELIX design docs, artifact templates, and phase guidance include a first-class contract artifact with explicit selection rules and naming guidance; contract template support exists in the workflow bundle; guidance covers non-HTTP protocol/interface contracts such as telemetry schemas; ADR/SD/TD/contract boundaries are unambiguous enough that a telemetry export contract can be placed without artifact-taxonomy confusion
    </acceptance>
    <labels>helix, phase:design, kind:spec, area:docs, area:workflow</labels>
    <metadata parent="helix-13cfe23f" spec-id="workflows/phases/02-design/README.md" base-rev="7f97067b3541319cac3ee3889691912844d95ae3" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="workflows/phases/02-design/README.md" path="workflows/phases/02-design/README.md"/>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="helix-1940a77b">
    <title>Add first-class contract artifact support in HELIX design phase</title>
    <description>
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Hugo version: 0.159.2 extended — pinned in .github/workflows/pages.yml · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading) · Agent harness: ddx agent run --harness claude — not claude -p</practices>
<governing>workflows/phases/02-design/README.md — HELIX design docs, artifact templates, and phase guidance include a first-class contract artifact with explicit selection rules and naming guidance</governing>
</context-digest>

HELIX design guidance already treats contracts as a first-class artifact category (`docs/helix/02-design/contracts/`), and the design gate expects contract docs to exist, but the workflow bundle does not currently ship a dedicated contract artifact template/prompt/meta alongside solution-design, technical-design, ADR, spike, and PoC templates. At the same time, live HELIX repos are using both `API-*` and `CONTRACT-*` identifiers for documents in the contracts directory. This leaves contract-shaped specs underspecified exactly when we need them for reusable protocol/interface definitions such as OpenTelemetry telemetry capture standards, DDx/HELIX boundary contracts, execution-document conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other cross-project schemas that should be shareable without dragging along feature-level or solution-level baggage.

Request: add an explicit first-class contract artifact type to HELIX design-phase tooling and documentation. The artifact must be suitable for normative external interface specifications, not just HTTP APIs. It should support protocol/data/interface contracts such as telemetry schemas, cross-project ownership boundaries, authoring conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other reusable contracts. The artifact definition should make clear that ADRs carry the decision/rationale (WHY), solution designs carry the feature-level approach (HOW for a feature), technical designs carry one bounded slice, and contracts carry the authoritative field-by-field/schema-level/interface-level specification that another team could implement against directly.

Requirements from this discussion:
1. Define clear selection rules for when to use ADR vs Solution Design vs Technical Design vs Contract.
2. Ship a contract template/prompt/meta in the HELIX workflow bundle.
3. Clarify identifier and naming guidance: whether `API-*` remains a subtype, whether `CONTRACT-*` is the canonical prefix, or whether both are supported with explicit scope boundaries.
4. Make the contract artifact suitable for non-HTTP surfaces, including telemetry/export contracts.
5. Require contracts to carry exact keys/fields, types, units, enums, precedence rules, formulas, error semantics, examples, and validation checklists where relevant.
6. Ensure the design gate/enforcer language matches the chosen artifact naming and template support.
7. Preserve the ability for contracts to be shared across projects without inheriting unrelated feature or solution baggage.
8. Document how contracts should be referenced from ADRs and solution designs so the normative schema lives in one place rather than being duplicated across artifacts.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
HELIX design docs, artifact templates, and phase guidance include a first-class contract artifact with explicit selection rules and naming guidance; contract template support exists in the workflow bundle; guidance covers non-HTTP protocol/interface contracts such as telemetry schemas; ADR/SD/TD/contract boundaries are unambiguous enough that a telemetry export contract can be placed without artifact-taxonomy confusion
    </acceptance>
    <labels>helix, phase:design, kind:spec, area:docs, area:workflow</labels>
    <metadata parent="helix-13cfe23f" spec-id="workflows/phases/02-design/README.md" base-rev="7f97067b3541319cac3ee3889691912844d95ae3" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T175755-d23c2a79"/>
  </bead>

  </governing>
</execute-bead>

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read file contents. Use instead of cat/head/tail.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

offset.description: 0-based line offset to start reading from

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return

tools.name: write
tools.description: Create or overwrite a file with the given content. Creates parent directories as needed.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file using exact string replacement.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

oldText.description: Exact text to find (must be unique in the file and must not overlap with other edits)

old_string.description: Legacy: exact string to find (use edits[] instead)

tools.name: bash
tools.description: Execute a shell command for targeted repo operations such as builds, tests, or git. Do not use this for file discovery or file reading; use the find, grep, ls, and read tools instead. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.

tools.name: find
tools.description: Find files by path pattern. Use this instead of shell find or ls when locating files by name.

pattern.description: Path pattern to match (e.g. '**/*.go', 'cmd/**/main.go'). Use ** to match across directories.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: grep
tools.description: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Use instead of grep/rg shell commands.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative or absolute; defaults to working directory)

glob.description: Restrict to files whose base names match this glob (e.g. '*.go', '*.ts')

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: ls
tools.description: List directory contents. Use instead of ls.

path.description: Directory to list (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

tools.name: patch
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file. Supports: replace (default), replace_all, prepend, and append operations.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

search.description: Exact text to find in the file. Must match exactly (including whitespace). Will match first occurrence.

content.description: Replacement text, or text to prepend/append

operation.description: One of: 'replace' (default, search must be unique), 'replace_all' (replace every occurrence), 'prepend' (insert at file start), 'append' (insert at file end)

tools.name: task
tools.description: Track subtasks in a multi-step plan: create, update, get, list.

id.description: Task ID (required for update/get, ignored for create/list)
